title: Bleeder type engine brake with hydraulic-mechanical actuation and lash adjustment
abstract: A bleeder brake mechanism (30) includes a carrier (32), a latch mechanism (40) and a lost motion assembly (50) and is configured to selectively cooperate with an exhaust valve (82) during an engine braking event. The exhaust valve (82) is associated with an exhaust valve rocker arm. The carrier (32) defines an oil gallery. The latch mechanism (40) has a latch that is selectively actuated independently of rocker arm movement between a first non-actuated position and a second actuated position based on an oil pressure in the oil gallery. The lost motion assembly (50) is disposed in the carrier (32) and has a plunger (70), a collar (72) and a plunger biasing member (74). The latch is configured to move between the first non-actuated position wherein at least one of the collar and plunger is permitted to undergo lost motion and the second actuated position.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1 . A bleeder brake mechanism configured to selectively cooperate with an exhaust valve during an engine braking event, the exhaust valve associated with an exhaust valve rocker arm, the bleeder brake mechanism comprising: a carrier defining an oil gallery; a latch mechanism having a latch that is selectively actuated independently of rocker arm movement between a first non-actuated position and a second actuated position based on an oil pressure in the oil gallery; and a lost motion assembly disposed in the carrier and having a plunger, a collar and a plunger biasing member; wherein the latch is configured to move between the first non-actuated position wherein at least one of the collar and plunger is permitted to undergo lost motion and the second actuated position wherein at least one of the collar and plunger is precluded from translating away from the exhaust valve thereby positioning the exhaust valve at least partially open. 2 The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 1 wherein the plunger forces the exhaust valve open in the second actuated position. 3. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 1 , further comprising a latch pin biasing member that biases the latch pin toward the first non-actuated position. 4. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 3, further comprising a set screw that caps off a bore defined in the carrier that houses the latch mechanism. 5. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 1 wherein the latch mechanism is hydraulically actuated into the second actuated position. 6. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 1 wherein the lost motion assembly provides mechanical actuation. 7. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 1 wherein the plunger biasing member biases the plunger toward the exhaust valve. 8. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 7 wherein the plunger and collar are fixed for concurrent translation relative to the exhaust valve during normal engine operation. 9. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 7 wherein the plunger is threaded relative to the collar whereby the plunger can be rotated relative to the collar for lash adjustment. 10. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 1 , further comprising a bridge having a bridge sliding pin extending therefrom. 11. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 10 wherein the plunger is in contact with the bridge sliding pin. 12. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 1 wherein the bleeder brake mechanism is contained on the carrier that is mounted on a fuel injector support. 13. A bleeder brake mechanism configured to selectively cooperate with an exhaust valve during an engine braking event, the exhaust valve associated with an exhaust valve rocker arm, the bleeder brake mechanism comprising: a carrier defining an oil gallery; a hydraulically actuated latch mechanism having a latch that is selectively actuated independently of exhaust valve rocker arm movement between a first non-actuated position wherein a latch pin biasing member biases the latch pin toward the first non-actuated position, and a second actuated position wherein high pressure oil enters the oil gallery causing the latch to overcome the latch pin biasing member and translate into the actuated position; and a lost motion assembly disposed in the carrier and having a plunger, a collar and a plunger biasing member; 

 wherein the latch is configured to move between the first non-actuated position wherein the plunger is permitted to undergo lost motion and the second actuated position wherein the plunger is precluded by the latch from translating away from the exhaust valve thereby positioning the exhaust valve at least partially open during the engine braking event. 14. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 13 wherein the lost motion assembly provides mechanical actuation. 15. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 13 wherein the plunger biasing member biases the plunger toward the exhaust valve. 16. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 15 wherein the plunger and collar are fixed for concurrent translation relative to the exhaust valve during normal engine operation. 17. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 15 wherein the plunger is threaded relative to the collar whereby the plunger can be rotated relative to the collar for lash adjustment. 18. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 13, further comprising a bridge having a bridge sliding pin extending therefrom. 19. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 18 wherein the plunger is in contact with the bridge sliding pin. 20. The bleeder brake mechanism of claim 13 wherein the bleeder brake mechanism is contained on the carrier that is mounted on a fuel injector support.
